summary: A problematic audience tape with channel swaps, clipping and dropouts, yet it delivers a high‑energy Deal and a memorable Morning Dew.
review: The show suffers from flubby playing and numerous technical flaws in the AUD sources—channel reversals, clipped beginnings and a noisy, echoey mix—but a few moments shine through, notably an exciting Deal jam, a superb Scarlet Begonias, and arguably the year’s best Morning Dew. Overall the recording is hard to enjoy, reflected in the low rating.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review highlights both poor sound quality and uneven performance, leading to a combined low score (≈2.3) despite a few standout songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Channels swapped on first set, clipped Althea intro, extensive dropouts and chewed tape from the second set onward, echoey venue acoustics, high noise floor and reduced dynamics.

show_quality.setlist_flow: An uptempo, two‑hour second set with a long jam sequence; overall the performance feels sloppy and flubby, but occasional high‑energy peaks keep it from being a total loss.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia plays better than usual, leading the Deal jam and generally staying on point, though his vocals slip on Touch of Grey.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh struggles throughout—loses place in Jack Straw, has vocal issues, muffles lyrics and contributes to a generally uneven performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (Keith) adds quirky riffs in Scarlet Begonias and thematic bits in Fire on the Mountain, but overall keyboard work is inconsistent.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drums>Space segment is weak, with modest improvement during the Playing in the Band reprise.
